sections.paragraph:
πολυπλήθεια, ας, ἡ (πολύς, πλῆθος; Hippocr. et al.; Menand., Dysc. 166; Περὶ ὕψους 32, 1 p. 56, 5 V.; Ps.-Plut., Hom. 85; Dit., Syll. 880, 40; 2 Macc 8:16) large crowd Ac 14:7 v.l. (in an addition in codex E; codex D has πλῆθος). τὴν π. (πολυπληθίαν is to be rejected, w. Lghtf., and πολυπλήθειαν, which is also attested, is to be inserted) ὑμῶν ἀπείληφα I have received your whole (large) congregation IEph 1:3.—DELG s.v. πίμπλημι.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Ps.-Plut Ps.-Plut , Vi(ta) Hom(eri), II a.d.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 847
